By David Pierson,Berry Wang,and Bhadra Sharma NEW YORK TIMES

HONG KONG – Using their ands and s i litions, rescue workers dug hrough the rubble in the earch for survivors after a leadly 7.1 magnitude earhuake toppled houses and joltd people awake in a remote f   he northern foothills of Mount Everest.

At least 126 people have lied and 188 were injured in he quake, which struck shortly fter 9 a.m. at a depth of 6.2niles inDingriCounty,near ne of Tibet's most historic citlia reported.The quake was he country's deadliest since December 2023, when 151 peole were killed in a 6.2 magniude temblor in the northwestrn provinces of Gansu and inghai.
c  China's state broadcaster reorted that more than 1,000ouses had experienced some ormofd amage in Dingri ounty, where the average altiude is around 15,000 et,long the Himalayan border ihNeal

T eing conducted without heavy quipment, underscoring the hallenge of dlivering resours to the lagly iolated comnuniti es dam aged by the uake. With temperatures in  g  egrees Fahrenheit, rescue  t hich to locate survivors.It   nany esidens had been eft omeless.
Several aftershocks wee lt ntherig in epal.he quake had a magnitude of .1, according to the US Geooical rvy tugh neasureda6.ythh R
The nearest city to te uake's epicenter was Shigatse,he -i  ,

with a population of 640,000.Shigatse is home to the vast,centuries-old Tashilhunpo Monastery, the traditional seat of the Panchen Lama, one of the most senior figures in Tibetan Buddhism. State media  said no damage to the monastery had been reported so far.
Scenes of destruction were broadcast on state media and shared on social media. A tourist not far from Shigatse who  spoke to The New York Times

said she was in her hotel room when the earthquake started shaking her building. She said the electricity went out and  that she and a friend had squatted between the beds. When the shaking stopped, they ran out of the building.

The tourist, who only gave her surname, Xu, shared a video showing several single-story brick buildings with collapsed walls. Video posted on Chinese social media showed streets strewed with rubble,cars crushed by fallen bricks, and roads split open by the shifed ground. Xu said she grabbed her down jacket before she ran out.

China's top leader, Xi Jinping, ordered officials to mini mize casualties and resettle survivors. Chinese authorities deployed 3,400 rescuers and more than 340 medical workers for the search effort, and dis

司fected by bottlenecks caused by damaged roads, said Robert Barnett, a professor from SOAs University of London, who has visited the region and described it as looking like "vast moonscape."
E "This is harsh, high-altitude land," Barnett said. "The roads are quite few and susceptible to landslides."
That said, Barnett added that there could be new roads connecting some of Dingri County's most remote border villages built in recent years to assert China's sovereignty along its periphery.

1S the earthquake, there could be some damage in mountains of eastern Nepal,," said Lok Bijaya  Adhikari, a senior seismologist at Nepal's National Earthquake Monitoring and Research Center

添司ministration in its final days is shifting more than $100 million in military aid from Israel and Egypt to Lebanon as it tries to bolster a cease-fire agreement it helped mediate between Israel and Hezbollah.

In separate notices sent to Congress, the State Department said it was moving $95million in military assistance intended for Egypt and $7.5 million for Israel toward sup porting the Lebanese army and its government. The notices were dated Jan. 3 and obtained by the Associated Press on

Most of the money will go to the Lebanese Armed Forces,which have a critical role in standing up the cease-fire that was agreed to in November following an all-out war between Israeli and Hezbollah that battered much of southern and eastern Lebanon for two months.

It is intended to help the LAF deploy in the south of the country and supplement the role of the UN peacekeeping mission patrolling the so-called Blue Line, which has separated Israel and Lebanon since the end of a monthlong Israel-Hezbollah war in 2006.
Both Israel and Hezbollah agreed to pull their forces out of southern Lebanon before
pliance to be overseen by the Lebanese army and UN peacekeepers.

"US security assistance to the LAF increases its capacity as the country's only legitimate  military force and defender of Lebanon's territorial integrity,enables the LAF to prevent potential destabilization from ISIS and other terrorist groups,and enables the LAF to provide  security both for the Lebanese  people and for US personnel,"the State Department said.

Pro-Israel members of Congress and others have in the  past complained about any diversion of US assistance to Israel, although it was not immediately clear if there would be objections to such a small amount of shifted money.

At the same time, some of those who have been forceful advocates of Israel and critics of US assistance to the Lebanese military have often complained that it has been infiltrated by Hezbollah. The notices rejected that claim.

"US support to the LAF reinforces the LAF as an important institutional counterweight to Hezbollah, which receives weapons, training, and financial support from Iran,"the State Department said."The LAF continues to be an independent, non-sectarian institution in Lebanon, and is respected across all sectors."

In a third notice, also sent to Congress on Jan. 3, the department said it was going to provide $15 million to Lebanon's Internal Security Forces to ensure that they become the  primary law enforcement entity in the country and assist the LAF in controlling areas in the
DaILY BRieFING
France marks 10th anniversary of terror attack

PARIS –A ee aer Islamic terrorists stormed the offices of the satirical newspa per Charlie Hebdo in a deadly assault that shook France to its core and ignited a global outcry in defense of freedom of speech, the nation paused on Tuesday to honor the victims and renew its resolve to fight for liberty and democracy.
cron and Paris Mayor Anne Hidalgo led solemn tributes at the  site of the attack, where 12 people, including some of France's most beloved cartoonists, were killed on Jan. 7, 2015.
was Ahmed Merabet, a police officer who was gunned down on the street while defending the newspaper.

公司添Macron stood with his wife,Brigitte, alongside former pres ident François Hollande, who had steered France through the aftermath of the atack. Macror  joined pollice officers in laying wreaths of flowers against the wall of the former Charlie Heb do headquarters in the 11th district and the gathering observed a minute of silence.司

Nine workers  trapped in mine  in northern India

GUWAHATI, India – At least nine workers are trapped inside a fl ooded coal mine in India's northeastern Assam state, offficials said Tuesday, as authorities  summoned the army to help in the rescue operation.
司a on Monday morning in the Umrangso area in Dima Hasao district, about 125 miles south of the state capital, Guwahati.
The workers are feared trapped 300 feet below the ground after water gushed in from a nearby unused mine.
"We are mobilizing resources to rescue them," said Kaushik Rai, a government minister.
Army soldiers and a disaster team used ropes and cranes in the operation. Rescuers found three helmets, some slippers,and a few other items, Rai said.
"The divers have been able t dive into 35 or 40 feet of water inside the mine," he said. "The water level now is estimated at 100 feet."
Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma said that the mine appeared to be illegal, and that police had arrested one per  son thus far.
ASSOCIATED PRESS
S. Korea court issues new arrest warrant for Yoon

SEOUL – South Korea's anticorruption  e received a nw court warrant on Tuesday to detain impeached President Yoon uk eol aer ts previous attempt was blocd b the presidential security service last week.

The Corrupon Investigatio Office for High-Ranking Off icials, which plans to question the embattled president on rebellion allegations over his short-lived martial law decree on Dec. 3, didn't confirm how long the warrant would remain valid. Chief prosecutor Oh Dong-woon refused to answer when asked by lawmakers whe the warrant would expire, saying the infrmatn is sensitive
司a last seven days but can be extended to around 10 days. Oh didn't say when investigators planned to make their next attempt to detain Yoon.

The Seoul Western District Court last week had initially is sued a warrant to detain Yoon and a separate warrant to sear his residence after he repeated defied authorities by refusing appear for questioning.
